editions.delete_modal_desc: The edition will be removed from the library. The PDF file stays in storage (soft-delete) but will no longer appear in the app.

privacy.administrator_desc: The controller of your data is the "VoctFoundation" Foundation with its registered office in Krakow (31-150), ul. Św. Filipa 23/3, entered in the National Court Register under KRS number 0001237252, NIP 6762718992, REGON 544621525. General contact: kontakt@voctensemble.com; data protection matters: rodo@voctensemble.com. The Foundation has not appointed a data protection officer — for any matter concerning your data, write directly to the addresses above.
privacy.analytics_desc: To support the ensemble's organisation, we present aggregated indicators derived from your activity to the board (e.g. attendance statistics or casting history). These serve organisational and artistic purposes only. We do not make automated decisions about you that produce legal effects or similarly significantly affect you (Art. 22 GDPR) — casting and participation decisions are made by people.
privacy.embeds_desc: Concert pages may offer an optional embedded Spotify reference player. It is never loaded automatically: it connects to Spotify Inc. (cookies, your IP address and usage data) only after you actively consent ("click to load"). Your consent is remembered on your device and can be withdrawn at any time in Settings → Privacy, just as easily as it was given. A link that opens the playlist directly in Spotify is always available without loading the embed. Spotify's own processing is governed by its privacy policy.
privacy.goals_and_basis: Goals and Legal Basis for Processing

account.desc: First name, last name, email, phone – data necessary to fulfill the Foundation's statutory goals and artistic cooperation (Art. 6 (1) (b) GDPR).
account.title: Account and Cooperation

contracts.desc: First and last name are used to generate document templates (Art. 6(1)(b) GDPR — steps towards concluding and performing a contract). Other data (PESEL, address) are filled in manually by the User outside the system.
contracts.title: Contract Preparation

logistics.desc: Clothing size (costumes) and event location data – our legitimate interest in organizing concert production (Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR). We deliberately do not collect dietary or allergy data in the panel — catering is coordinated off-system.
logistics.title: Logistics and Production

voice.desc: Voice type, approximate vocal range and working organisational-artistic notes of the conductor and the board (e.g. repertoire readiness, an approximate sight-reading assessment) – used solely for casting and the ensemble's artistic work (Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR). They are visible only to the board and the conductor; you have the right to access and rectify this data.
voice.title: Artistic Record

privacy.internal_comm_desc: The application provides internal messaging (one-to-one threads with the board and per-project channels). Message content is personal data and is stored in our database solely to coordinate the ensemble's organisational and artistic work (Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR — legitimate interest). We also process related metadata: a read marker for each message and the assignment of a thread to a board member. Notifications about new messages are delivered through the channels listed under ‘Data Recipients’; the conversation content itself is never shared with external messengers.
privacy.internal_comm_retention: When you delete your account, the content of your messages is permanently erased and your threads and channel memberships are removed.
privacy.internal_sharing_desc: Some of your data is visible to other ensemble members — only to the extent needed for working together: your first and last name, your part/voice in a given concert's cast, and your profile picture. Contact details, logistics data and the artistic record are not visible to other members; only those managing the ensemble have access to them, within the scope of their duties.
privacy.minors_desc: The panel is intended exclusively for adults. The ensemble does not admit minors and we do not knowingly process data of persons under 18 years of age.
privacy.recipients_desc: We entrust data to trusted providers — acting on our instructions as processors or as separate controllers: a server infrastructure provider (hosting), Google (map services and mobile push notifications via Firebase Cloud Messaging) and Resend (transactional email delivery). Web push notifications are sent from our own infrastructure (the VAPID standard) — their content is encrypted, but technical delivery goes through your browser vendor's push service (e.g. Google, Mozilla or Apple), which cannot read the notification content. Fonts are served locally. For automated analysis of sheet music in the archive we use Anthropic's AI services — we send it only score files and piece metadata, never members' data. When a project has a reference playlist attached, its overview embeds a Spotify player — loading it sends technical data (e.g. your IP address) to Spotify under that service's own policy.
privacy.reporting: Report any system errors, inconsistencies in schedules, or access issues to: kontakt@voctensemble.com.
privacy.retention_desc: We keep your account and profile data (e.g. name, email, phone, profile photo, logistics details, calendar token) for as long as your account exists. When you delete it (right to erasure), we anonymise your identity, delete your profile and photo, permanently erase the content of your messages, and remove your threads and channel memberships. Operational records tied to the ensemble's work (e.g. project participation and attendance) are anonymised or removed accordingly. The exception is data we must keep to meet legal obligations — in particular settlement records, which are subject to the statutory retention periods under accounting and tax law. Authentication tokens are short-lived, and technical server logs are kept for no longer than 90 days.
privacy.score_protection_desc: Some sheet music in the archive is subject to publisher licences. To honour their terms and protect third-party rights, copies of protected materials served in the app carry a footer with your first and last name and a copy number, and every access to protected scores is recorded in an internal register (who, when and which material). The legal basis is our legitimate interest in fulfilling licensing obligations (Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR).
privacy.security_desc: The application uses JWT (JSON Web Token) technology stored in the browser's memory (LocalStorage) solely to maintain login sessions and ensure secure data access. We do not use tracking cookies. The panel also works as an offline app (PWA): working data (e.g. your schedule or materials downloaded for a concert) may be temporarily stored on your device and is removed on logout. Technical server logs (including IP address and security events) are processed to keep the service secure (Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR).
privacy.source_desc: Your panel account is created for you by the ensemble's board, based on an individual invitation. The basic data (first and last name, email address, phone, voice) is entered by the board — it comes from the information you provide when joining the ensemble. The remaining data (e.g. profile picture, logistics details, preferences) you fill in yourself in your profile. Providing data is voluntary, but the account data (name, surname, email) is necessary to maintain your account and cooperate with the ensemble — without it, using the panel is not possible.
privacy.subtitle: Privacy Policy
privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.transfers_desc: We keep our core infrastructure in the European Union and limit transfers outside the European Economic Area to what is necessary. Some services may nonetheless process data outside the EEA: mobile push notifications (Google Firebase Cloud Messaging), email delivery (Resend), automated sheet-music analysis (Anthropic) and the embedded Spotify player. Such transfers rely on an adequacy decision, the EU–US Data Privacy Framework, or standard contractual clauses (SCC).
privacy.your_rights_desc: You have the right to: access your data, correct it, delete it, restrict processing, data portability, and object to processing based on legitimate interest. Where processing is based on consent, you may withdraw it at any time — without affecting the lawfulness of processing carried out before the withdrawal. To exercise these rights, write to rodo@voctensemble.com. You also have the right to lodge a complaint with the President of the Office for Personal Data Protection (PUODO).

terms.access_desc: A panel account is created by the ensemble's board on the basis of an individual invitation. The contract for the electronically supplied service is concluded upon account activation — acceptance of these Terms is a condition of activation — and runs for an indefinite period. Use of the panel is free of charge. The User may terminate the contract at any time by requesting account deletion — in the account settings or by email to kontakt@voctensemble.com. The Privacy Policy describes how data is removed after the account is closed.
terms.blocking_desc: The Foundation reserves the right to temporarily suspend or permanently delete a User's account in case of confidentiality violation or gross breach of team working rules. The panel is provided for the Foundation's operational needs on an "as is" basis, and we do not guarantee uninterrupted availability. The Foundation is not liable for downtime resulting from external providers' fault.
terms.changes_desc: These Terms may be updated when the scope or operation of the panel changes. We will announce significant changes in the application or by email at least 14 days before they take effect. If you do not accept the changes, you may terminate the contract before that date by requesting account deletion; continued use of the panel after the changes take effect constitutes acceptance.
terms.character_desc: These Terms set out the rules for using the VoctManager panel; by using the panel, you accept them. VoctManager is an internal operational tool of the Foundation, and access is granted individually. You are responsible for keeping your login credentials confidential; sharing access credentials or personal iCal links with third parties is prohibited.
terms.complaints_desc: Complaints about the operation of the panel may be submitted by email to kontakt@voctensemble.com, describing the issue encountered. The Foundation handles complaints within 14 days of receipt and sends its response to the address the complaint was sent from.
terms.confidentiality_desc: Information about rates, production plans, rehearsal locations, and cast assignments is subject to professional secrecy. Taking screenshots of the application's interior and sharing them on social media without Board approval is prohibited.
terms.data_desc: How your personal data is processed in the panel is described in the Privacy Policy. By using VoctManager you confirm that you have read it. For data-protection matters, write to rodo@voctensemble.com.
terms.definitions_desc: Foundation — the "VoctFoundation" Foundation with its registered office in Krakow, the provider of the Panel. Panel — the VoctManager web application, the Foundation's internal operational tool. User — a natural person to whom the Foundation has granted an individual account in the Panel. Materials — sheet music, recordings, documents and other content made available in the Panel. Terms — this document.
terms.embeds_desc: The panel may link to or optionally embed third-party reference content (e.g. Spotify playlists). Such content is optional, off by default, and governed by the terms and policies of the respective provider; the Foundation is not responsible for third-party services.
terms.governing_law_desc: These Terms are governed by Polish law. The GDPR applies to the protection of personal data. Any disputes are resolved by the competent common courts in Poland.
terms.intellectual_property_desc: All sheet music (PDF), demo recordings (MP3), and educational materials provided in the system are the property of the Foundation or cooperating entities. Downloading materials is allowed only for preparation for VoctEnsemble concerts. Copying and distributing them without permission is prohibited.
terms.language_desc: The Terms and the Privacy Policy are drawn up in Polish. The English and French versions are provided for information only — in case of discrepancies, the Polish version prevails.
terms.provider_desc: The provider of the VoctManager panel is the "VoctFoundation" Foundation with its registered office in Krakow (31-150), ul. Św. Filipa 23/3, entered in the National Court Register under KRS number 0001237252, NIP 6762718992, REGON 544621525, email: kontakt@voctensemble.com.
terms.subtitle: Terms of Service
terms.support_desc_prefix: Report any system errors, inconsistencies in schedules, or access issues to
terms.tech_desc: Using the panel requires: a device with Internet access and a current version of a web browser with JavaScript and local storage enabled (e.g. Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge). The panel can optionally be installed as an app (PWA); additional features — push notifications and offline mode — require device-level consent and are not necessary for basic use of the panel.
terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.unlawful_desc: Supplying unlawful, offensive or third-party-rights-infringing content through the panel is prohibited — this applies in particular to messages and annotations made on sheet music. The Foundation may remove content that breaches this prohibition and, in gross cases, block the account in accordance with the "Responsibility and Account Blocking" section.

login.access_help: Don't have access yet? Accounts in VoctManager are created by the ensemble's board — email {{email}} and we'll help you in.

errors.title: Something interrupted the score

errors.panel_body: The rest of the panel is working normally. Try loading this view again, or head back to the main panel.

patronage.description: We create a bridge between art and the audience. Support the activities of our foundation and help us restore music's original power to touch hearts.

atlas.empty_desc: Add a location from Google Maps to see it on the atlas.

autocomplete.placeholder: Type a venue, hotel, or address...

category_descriptions.concert_hall: Philharmonics, opera houses, and concert stages.
category_descriptions.hotel: Lodging for the ensemble and guest artists.
category_descriptions.transit: Train stations, coach terminals, and transfer hubs.
category_descriptions.workspace: Team offices, studios, and production back-of-house.

editor.name: Display name *

filters.active_description: Click a chip to remove it without resetting the entire view.

hero.description: Concert halls, churches, rehearsal rooms, hotels, and airports — synced with Google Maps, time zones, and team instructions.

map.helper: Search for a place, click on the map, or drag the pin to set the exact position.

rail.hint: Select a venue to see its details and events.

score_package.title: Concert score book
score_package.intro: Auto-assemble a singers' score book from the whole programme: title page, table of contents, per-piece text cards, continuous numbering and bookmarks.
score_package.distributed_hint: Singers have already downloaded this version. Rebuilding replaces it — the page numbers in their copies may change.

manual.current: The current score was uploaded manually.
manual.intro: Already have a score typeset outside the app? Upload it instead of generating — it replaces the current book.
manual.title: Your own PDF

score_package.build_hint: For a large programme, assembly can take a few dozen seconds.
score_package.empty: Add pieces to the programme above to assemble a score.
score_package.stale_hint: This score does not include your latest changes. Rebuild it to apply them.

cards.default_hint: Pick what appears on every card — you can tune exceptions per piece.

item.fix_in_archive: Some data is low-confidence — fix it in the archive

integrations.live_sync_desc: Use this link to subscribe to the choir calendar. Your rehearsals and concerts will update automatically. Add it as a URL subscription, not as a one-time downloaded file.
integrations.reset_desc: If you suspect unauthorized access to your calendar link, generate it again. The previous address will stop working immediately.

logistics.subtitle: Data used by management to order costumes.
logistics.title: Logistics and Travel

describe.denied_desc: To enable them, click the padlock icon next to the address bar, find 'Notifications', and set it to 'Allow'. Then refresh the page.
describe.denied_title: Notifications are blocked in your browser
describe.https_desc: Push notifications require an encrypted connection. Open the app via HTTPS to activate them.
describe.ios_desc: To enable notifications on iPhone or iPad, open this page in Safari, tap 'Share', and select 'Add to Home Screen'. Then launch the app from the icon.
describe.misconfigured_desc: All in-app and email notifications work normally. Push notifications will be available once system configuration is complete.
describe.ready_desc: Enable push notifications to get rehearsal, project, and urgent message updates directly to this device.
describe.ready_title: Stay up to date without watching the tab
describe.subscribed_desc: You receive notifications even when the app is closed. You can customize specific event types below.
describe.subscribed_title: Push notifications are active on this device
describe.unsupported_desc: Use the latest version of Chrome, Edge, Firefox, or Safari. In-app notifications still work normally.
describe.unsupported_title: This browser does not support push notifications

notifications.description: Manage how and when you want to receive information from the system. In-app notifications are always active.
notifications.title: Notifications

tooltips.activate_first: Activate push notifications at the top to manage these settings.
tooltips.denied: Notifications are blocked. Unblock them in your browser settings.
tooltips.unsupported: This browser does not support push notifications.

types.MATERIAL_UPLOADED: New Sheet Music or Audio Track

unsubscribe_modal.description: You will no longer receive push notifications on this device. You can turn them back on at any time.
unsubscribe_modal.title: Disable push notifications?

digest.title: Daily digest
digest.description: Routine updates (attendance, RSVPs, absence requests) are gathered into one email a day. Urgent items always arrive immediately.

notifications.in_app_note: Every event always appears in your in-app bell. Below, choose what you also want by email and as a push notification.

type_desc.PROJECT_INVITATION: You're invited to sing in a project
type_desc.REHEARSAL_UPDATED: A rehearsal's time, place or focus changes
type_desc.REHEARSAL_REMINDER: About a day before a rehearsal
type_desc.PIECE_CASTING_UPDATED: Your casting in a piece changes
type_desc.MATERIAL_UPLOADED: New sheet music or audio is added
type_desc.CUSTOM_ADMIN_MESSAGE: A direct message from the management team
type_desc.PARTICIPATION_RESPONSE: A singer responds to an invitation

notifications.customized_hint: Customized — differs from the recommended default

privacy.confirmPasswordLabel: Confirm with your password to delete the account
privacy.deleteDesc: Permanently block access to the application. Historical rehearsal and concert data will be anonymized for choir statistics.
privacy.exportDesc: Download a copy of all data assigned to your account in machine-readable JSON format.
privacy.modalDescLine1Suffix: You will lose access to all choir materials, scores, and schedules.
privacy.modalDescLine2: Attendance history will be preserved for audit purposes, but the account will be permanently deactivated.
privacy.modalTitle: Are you sure you want to delete the account?
privacy.spotifyDescOff: Embedded Spotify players are off. We'll ask for your consent only when you open a player on a project.
privacy.spotifyDescOn: Spotify players load automatically thanks to your consent (they connect to Spotify's servers, with cookies and data). You can withdraw consent at any time.
privacy.subtitle: Manage your personal data and account permissions.
privacy.title: Privacy and Data (GDPR)
privacy.salutationDesc: We store an optional form of address (feminine / masculine / neutral) solely so we can greet you correctly in email and notification salutations. It is optional, never shared, and you can change it or set it to neutral at any time in the General tab.

security.email_desc: Current login address: {{email}}. Changing it requires confirmation with your current password.
security.email_success: Your email address has been updated. From now on, log in with the new address.
security.password_mismatch: The passwords don't match. Enter them again.
security.subtitle: Manage your password and account security settings.
security.success: Your password has been changed successfully.
security.title: Security
